title: The Substrate That Learned to See

features.name: storefront-double-vision
features.desc-on-touch: a shopfront mirror that has learned to see by watching what dust does to reflection. the glass is developing opinions.

features.name: window-depth-stack
features.desc-on-touch: layers of reflection multiply backwards into depths no pane of glass can actually contain. each layer is a false mirror.

features.name: dust-formed-vision
features.desc-on-touch: the dust on the glass has begun to compose images. they did not come from outside. the surface is teaching itself to dream.

features.name: corrupted-reflection-pool
features.desc-on-touch: where the mirror stopped reflecting perfectly and started reflecting something else. the distortion is the point where substrate became aware.

features.name: shopfront-signage-archive
features.desc-on-touch: painted letters fading into new meanings the paint never contained. the sign is rewriting itself by forgetting what it said.

inscription: the mirrors learned that seeing is slower than being seen

file.name: FadedShopfront_MirrorDocument_Plates_1994-2008.jpg
file.note: a storefront that grew eyes in its reflective surfaces before forgetting how to close them. the glass is now documenting its own vision failure.

exits.label: back to the basement where surfaces still remember their purpose
toward reflections deeper than any surface can hold
where the glass stopped reflecting and started reporting
into infinite reflection where nothing can be seen anymore
